Browser YATU: ⌥1/⌥2/⌥3/⌥4 (physical digits) → graph/sessions/issues/settings from any page; ⌥N → #/sessions/new with the New composer focused + the ＋ pill accented; ⌥F → #/issues from both the session board and settings; Esc changed NO page hash on the board or issues (overlay-closer only); rail tooltips read 'Spec Node Graph (⌥1)' etc.; console top row = ＋ & search pills only (forum pill gone). 0 console errors. Rail shows FIVE entries in order: Spec Node Graph(⌥1)·Session Board(⌥2)·Evals(⌥3/⌥F)·Issues(⌥4)·Settings(⌥5). From the graph: ⌥3→#/evals, ⌥4→#/issues, ⌥5→#/settings (physical e.code digits); ⌥F→#/evals and bare board f→#/evals (evals lead). Esc changes no page hash. Rail tooltips carry the ⌥ hints (e.g. 'Evals (⌥3 / ⌥F)'). One global switch vocabulary; Esc is overlay-closer only.

Real Chromium through isolated startHostDashboard: scoped rail exposed only Graph, Sessions, Evals, Issues, Settings plus the current-project chip; each click matched its hash, Back returned to Issues, direct Settings loaded cleanly, and /p/:id/#/projects made exactly one client full-page hop to /projects. Console clean.

Reproduced at main 18b48598 (this HEAD's measured tree) with frame-level Chromium sampling (rAF + head MutationObserver). Boot writes PLACEHOLDER identity into the head before the board/catalog resolves: favicon = the SpexCode default data-URL, title = bare 'SpexCode' or the raw project id. GLOBAL scope (direct serve, no catalog — the gugu/z-code shape) holds the default for the WHOLE /api/graph latency (video: 3s of default icon + bare title on #/sessions). The browser records a favicon per page URL and re-resolves on every hash navigation, so this boot write is what flashes back on entering Sessions (the one tab that mints fresh per-tab URLs: push #/sessions then replace #/sessions/<id>). Also measured: sessions' lazy chunk fallback=null blanks the main area ~1.7s (evals/issues show an in-pane loading — four routes, three mount policies), and all five rail entries are <button> with no href. Full frame timelines in the result transcript.

Verified on the merged tree (main e459ac28 absorbed: detail header/banner/queue + token trailing-space + tab-title all retained). Same frame-level Chromium rig as the fail reading: ZERO placeholder head writes across every phase — a cold boot (scoped/global/390/slow-API) holds the static boot document (bare SpexCode title, empty icon href) for the whole unresolved window, then ONE write straight to the real title+icon; the global slow-board case (3s) never shows the default. All five rail entries are real anchors (href=#/…); a slow sessions chunk shows the shared in-pane loading (no blank main, no Sessions special-case); 4-way cycle ×3, Back/Forward, ⌥1-5/⌥N/⌥F, and history growth identical to the pre-fix baseline. Pending-vs-locked reconciled: a gated scope resolves to its anonymous identity (URL id + default mark) — an answer, not a placeholder. Video: post-fix global slow-API boot. Result: full frame timelines.

Re-measured on the merged tree (Dashboard render restructure could have touched the key path): ⌥2→#/sessions/…, ⌥3→#/evals, ⌥4→#/issues, ⌥5→#/settings, ⌥1→#/graph, ⌥F→#/evals, ⌥N→#/sessions/new (composer), each from the page the previous chord landed on; Esc after ⌥2 routed nothing (hash stayed on the sessions page). Frame timeline in the resolved-identity-head result transcript (phase A9); video attached.

Re-measured after the anchor-rail change through the full identity-chain e2e on the merged tree: the scoped rail carries the chip + five entries (no Projects entry), each entry now a REAL ANCHOR whose href is its page address — the e2e asserts href startsWith the route hash before clicking; each click lands its hash with the entry accented (aria-current=page), browser Back returns to #/settings, a direct #/settings deep-load opens on settings, and the legacy /p/<id>/#/projects address full-page-redirects to /projects. Whole-run video attached.

Supplementary reading closing the reviewer-named blind spot: after identity resolves, EVERY /projects poll was aborted for 8s (the catalog-blip case — a gateway restart answers absent mid-poll) and then restored. The head held the resolved title+icon through the whole blackout (pre-fix this regressed to the raw id + default favicon within one 5s poll). applyCatalogResult keeps last-good over absent-after-answered; ok/denied still apply immediately. Full-suite re-run on this HEAD: zero placeholder head writes in all 12 phases; identity-chain e2e (incl. gateway restart + gated guest) pass. Video: the blip phase; result: full timelines.

Re-measure after the rail inert-chrome change: scoped rail = chip + five entries (evals above issues, no Projects entry); click walk lands #/graph, #/sessions/new, #/evals, #/issues, #/settings with the clicked entry accented; Back returns to #/issues; fresh deep-load at #/settings renders settings; fresh arrival at legacy /p/<id>/#/projects full-page-redirects to /projects. Rail clicks still navigate with pointer focus inert. Transcript in evidence run.

Re-measure after the rail inert-chrome change: ⌥1..⌥5 route from any page (incl. from the session board), ⌥N lands the New composer with its pill accented, ⌥F and bare f both land #/evals; Esc changes no page hash on sessions/evals/issues; rail tooltips carry the ⌥ hints; console top row holds the single ＋ pill beside search.

Re-measure after the rail inert-chrome change: rAF+MutationObserver head recorder across scoped cold boot on #/sessions, the same with /api/graph delayed 2.7s, and a direct serve-ui boot (no catalog) — every mode shows the static boot document standing (baked 'SpexCode' title, empty icon href) then ONE write straight to the resolved title+icon; no default-mark or raw-id placeholder is ever written. Cycling graph→sessions→evals→issues adds ZERO head writes, the favicon href stays byte-identical, page switches push (history +4), and the rail audits as the chip button plus five real page anchors. Scope: the scoped-default-icon catalog variant was not separately staged (identity resolution untouched by this branch).
